project.name: The Foundation Protocol
project.description: Decentralized content and compute protocol for global information access

maintainers.onboarding: See CONTRIBUTING.md — maintainer path starts with 3 merged PRs and 30-day active contribution

contribution_model.dco_note: All commits must be signed off with git commit -s (Developer Certificate of Origin)
contribution_model.first_contribution: Look for issues labelled 'good first issue' on GitHub

decision_process.major_changes: RFC issue (label: rfc) open for 14 days before merge
decision_process.security_fixes: Disclosed privately first — see SECURITY.md for responsible disclosure policy
decision_process.governance_amendments: Requires RFC + 2-week comment period + maintainer ratification

sustainability.funding_model: Community-supported, open to grants and sponsorships

accountability.succession_plan: Multi-sig control of critical repos; community fork rights guaranteed by Apache-2.0 license
accountability.fork_policy: Forking is explicitly encouraged — see LICENSE for terms

transparency.releases: Tagged on GitHub with signed commits
transparency.changelogs: Release notes published with each GitHub release
transparency.security_disclosures: Published in SECURITY.md after patch is available
